V-M-054.checks: Usage event, session, rollup, last-seen, and abuse-signal persistence is idempotent, bounded, restore-safe, and secret-free.
V-M-054.commands: pass-local 2026-05-20: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_mtproto_usage_telemetry.py tests/test_mtproto_ip_observability.py tests/test_db_migrations.py -v
V-M-054.evidence: Phase-43 backend wave passed with V-M-061 encrypted IP handoff, M-054 retention budget checks, migration metadata guard, and existing usage telemetry regressions.

verification-levels.module-local: Exercise Alembic migration metadata, repository idempotency, last-seen updates, session state transitions, rollup writes, and retention pruning with synthetic telemetry fixtures.
verification-levels.wave-level: Run with V-M-055 and V-M-056 so runtime ingestion writes M-054 rows and analytics can consume them without schema drift.
verification-levels.phase-level: Phase-42 cannot pass unless M-054 proves day, week, month, top-user, last-seen, traffic, error, and active-session evidence without storing raw secrets or full proxy links.
verification-levels.phase-43-level: Phase-43 requires 30-day raw event retention, 90-day session/history support, rollup preservation, and M-061 encrypted IP handoff without raw IP leakage in M-054 logs or general analytics.

phase-43-checks.check-1: Raw MTProto usage events older than 30 days are pruned only after rollup windows are preserved.
phase-43-checks.check-2: Session summaries remain available for 90 days so user detail can show duration, connection count, and last/current activity.
phase-43-checks.check-3: IP-bearing telemetry is normalized into hash/prefix fields in M-054 and delegated to M-061 for encrypted raw IP storage.
phase-43-checks.check-4: Retention jobs report deleted counts and storage-budget status without printing raw IPs or secrets.

module-checks.check-1: Migration creates event, session, rollup, last-seen, and abuse-signal storage with indexes needed by admin filters.
module-checks.check-2: runtime_event_id uniqueness makes batch ingestion idempotent and prevents duplicate traffic/session counts.
module-checks.check-3: Known assignment handshake updates last_seen and opens or refreshes an active session without storing SNI secrets.
module-checks.check-4: Close and error events end sessions, preserve duration, respect connection_count deltas, and increment safe error counters.
module-checks.check-5: Unknown and rejected SNI events store safe reason codes, masked SNI values, and no derived proxy secrets.
module-checks.check-6: Client address analytics use HMAC/hash or coarse prefix fields by default, not raw public IP values.
module-checks.check-7: Hourly, daily, weekly, and monthly rollups preserve byte counters, connection count, duration, and errors.
module-checks.check-8: Raw event retention is bounded while rollups remain available for admin reporting and future restore checks.

scenarios.scenario-1: Known assignment handshake updates last_seen and creates or updates a session without storing secrets.
scenarios.scenario-2: Unknown and rejected SNI events are stored with safe reason codes and masked identifiers.
scenarios.scenario-3: Hourly, daily, weekly, and monthly rollups preserve traffic, connection count, duration, and error counters.
scenarios.scenario-4: Duplicate runtime event ids do not double-count usage.
scenarios.scenario-5: Retention pruning deletes old raw events but leaves aggregate rollups intact.
scenarios.scenario-6: Phase-43 retention keeps three-month operational history through sessions, rollups, and M-061 IP observations while bounding raw event volume.

telemetry-requirements.field-runtime-event-id: Stable opaque id from runtime for idempotency.
telemetry-requirements.field-assignment-id: Known assignment id when SNI maps to an issued proxy.
telemetry-requirements.field-user-id: Safe internal user id when assignment is known.
telemetry-requirements.field-event-type: handshake, close, bytes, error, unknown_sni, rejected_sni, active_connection, or req_pq_proof.
telemetry-requirements.field-counters: bytes_in, bytes_out, duration_ms, connection_count, error_code, and observed_at.
telemetry-requirements.field-client-identity: ip_hash or ip_prefix only; raw public IP is excluded by default.
telemetry-requirements.field-phase43-raw-ip-handoff: Encrypted raw IP is outside M-054 storage and must be written through M-061 admin-only observability.

redaction-rules.rule-1: No full tg links, fake-TLS secrets, base secret, salt, runtime token, private keys, or full promotion tags.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Unknown SNI values must be masked before persistence and before logs.
redaction-rules.rule-3: Any fixture containing secret-like values must assert that repository outputs and logs redact them.

autonomous-execution-safety.safe-local: Local migrations and repository tests may run against disposable test databases.
autonomous-execution-safety.blocked-live: Live retention pruning or schema migration requires explicit operator-approved backup and maintenance window.
